hero.name: Guilherme Narciso
hero.tagline: Software Engineer @ Netcracker Technology | Java Expert | Spring Boot, React.js, Go
about.title: About Me
about.subtitle: 📍 São Paulo, Brazil · Fluent in English · Open to remote international opportunities
about.p1: Software Engineer with 3+ years of experience designing and building scalable, distributed systems. I specialize in Java (Spring Boot) with extensive experience working with microservices, event-driven architectures, and messaging systems such as RabbitMQ and Kafka. I have hands-on experience with observability and performance optimization in high-throughput systems.
about.p2: Currently at Netcracker (a NEC subsidiary, ~$2.7B revenue), developing OSS infrastructure for major telecom operators such as T-Mobile, Google Fiber, Rogers, and Claro. I have over 2 years of experience working in international teams, collaborating across different time zones, and delivering solutions for North America and Europe. Previously at Accenture, I contributed to backend solutions for large-scale clients like RaiaDrogasil and Circle K (Couche-Tard, 17,000+ stores across 29 countries).
about.p3: I'm comfortable owning complex problems end-to-end — from architectural decisions to production incident resolution. Recently, I identified and resolved a critical timeout issue during Google Fiber's go-live, reducing query execution time from 40+ seconds to under 6 seconds across datasets exceeding 3 million records.
about.p4: I also hold cloud certifications, including AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner and Microsoft Azure Fundamentals (AZ-900), reinforcing my expertise in cloud platforms and distributed system design.
experience.title: Experience
experience.netcracker.bullets.0: Part of the first Brazilian team selected for Netcracker's NDO OSS product (NEC subsidiary, ~$2.7B revenue), delivering backend features and critical fixes for global Tier 1 operators: T-Mobile, Google Fiber, Rogers, Telesat, Swisscom and Claro.
experience.netcracker.bullets.1: Prevented a critical production failure during Google Fiber's go-live by tracing a timeout bottleneck via Graylog and Kubernetes, then profiling and re-indexing an ArangoDB query in a Java microservice — reducing execution time from 40s+ to under 6s with 3M+ active records.
experience.netcracker.bullets.2: Backend development with Java (Spring Boot) and Go, including architecture design and technical decision-making prior to feature development, unit testing via JUnit and Mockito, Docker, Kubernetes, and CI/CD pipelines in an Agile Scrum team.
experience.accenture.bullets.0: Delivered backend features for RaiaDrogasil's platform — Brazil's leading pharmacy marketplace, serving 47.6M active customers — including event-driven microservices for the core order lifecycle: order processing, customer notifications, and inventory sync between RD and third-party sellers, via RabbitMQ with hundreds of async queues, Redis caching, OracleDB/MongoDB, and JUnit/Mockito test coverage.
experience.accenture.bullets.1: Developed the Click & Collect feature (buy online, pick up in store) for one of Brazil's largest pharmacy chains (2,800+ stores), integrating multiple payment providers and inventory systems via microservices.
experience.accenture.bullets.2: Built a Java socket server for real-time communication with fuel pumps at Circle K (Couche-Tard — 17,000+ stores in 29 countries), implementing an adapter pattern for 4+ pump models and an event orchestrator on cloud-native Azure (CosmosDB, Service Bus, AKS) with Azure Monitor observability.
experience.accenture.intern.bullets.0: Built a BFF in Node.js/NestJS with GraphQL for RaiaDrogasil's website and app, backed by a consolidated MongoDB layer aggregating multiple data sources — reducing catalog search response time from 10s+ to ~2s by eliminating fan-out calls to third-party providers.
experience.accenture.intern.bullets.1: Implemented event-driven microservices for the core order lifecycle of RaiaDrogasil's marketplace — order processing, customer notifications, and inventory sync — via RabbitMQ, Redis caching, and JUnit/Mockito test coverage.
